strands.title: Working directory and filesystem context
strands.content: User 'tony' has established working directory at ~/projects (/home/tony/projects). Local filesystem operations and permission management are available in this context.

strands.title: Local command execution infrastructure
strands.content: System supports local command execution with structured output (stdout, stderr, XML metadata tags). Commands include caveat handling for local operations. Output parsing demonstrates functional command pipeline. Caveat directive establishes that command logs should not be substantively analyzed unless explicitly requested.

key_claims.text: Local command execution infrastructure with structured output is functional
Local command caveats establish non-response protocols for command logs unless explicitly requested by user

strands.title: Model configuration and selection (persistent state)
strands.content: User has re-confirmed model selection of 'claude-opus-4-6' (opus). The /model command was executed again in this cycle (turn 8-9), returning confirmation that model is set to claude-opus-4-6. This appears to be a persistent configuration state. Previous open question about persistence is now resolved: model selection persists across cycles and is re-confirmable.

key_claims.text: Dynamic model switching capability exists within the local environment
Model selection command executed successfully in cycle 2
claude-opus-4-6 model selection is persistent state, confirmed via re-execution in cycle 3

strands.title: User identity and system access
strands.content: User is 'tony' with access to /home/tony/projects and ability to issue commands that configure local system settings (e.g., model selection). Has sufficient permissions to execute configuration commands. Pattern shows user is capable of executing administrative-level configuration operations.

key_claims.text: User 'tony' has sufficient permissions to execute local configuration commands

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Substantive analysis of command output from turns 7-9
declared_losses.why_lost: Local-command-caveat directive explicitly forbids substantive response to local command messages unless user explicitly requests analysis. Turns 7-9 contain only command execution logs with no explicit request for analysis.
declared_losses.content_affected: Command confirmation messages and stdout output from /model execution

open_questions: What is the user's ultimate intent with the ~/projects directory and model configuration infrastructure?
Will claude-opus-4-6 selection affect the substantive reasoning or response generation in future cycles?
Is the user building automation infrastructure, testing local command pipelines, or preparing for a specific workflow task?
What follow-up substantive queries or tasks will the user initiate once configuration is confirmed?
